text:
Accompany (, rom. Akanpanī) is a spell card found in the Greed Island game. It allows for the user and everyone within a 20 meter radius to be transported to a previously visited city or a previously met player of the caster's choice.
__NOTOC__

Summary

This card allows for the caster to be transported to a location within the game that the user has previously been to, or to a player that they have already met. Substantially, it combines the effects of "Return" and "Magnetic Force" and expands them to anyone within a 20 meter radius of the caster. The plurality of targets makes it a convenient spell when moving large parties or even to relocate oneself with one's enemy; for the same reason, when cast to escape from someone, the user must make sure they are more than 20 meters away from their pursuer.

Card Description

{| class="article-table" style="height: 10px; color: #1A004C; background-color: #EEFFEE" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" border="0"

|No.1039 :: 
呪文を使用したプレイヤーを含め、その半径２０ｍ以内にいるプレイヤー全てを指定した街（行ったことのある街に限る）か指定したプレイヤー（ゲーム内で出会ったことのあるプレイヤーに限る）のいる場所に飛ばす。
|-
![Eng]
|No.1039 :: Accompany 
Transports the caster to a place they have previously been at before (location). As it also transports the caster to previously met players as well. This card also works on multiple people within 20 m of the caster.
|}

Trivia

The player depicted in this card's illustration recurs in "Collision" and "Magnetic Force".
Furthermore, the illustration of the latter card is identical to that of "Accompany", except that it depicts a single player.
In Chapter 158 it is revealed that this spell, when used on 15 or more people with Soufrabi as the destination, triggers the event to obtain "Plot of Beach".
Gon uses a Mimic and a Paladin's Necklace to sneak an Accompany card out of the game so he can transport himself and Killua to the G.I. player listed as "N***".
In Chapter 185 it is revealed that Ging had Elena program the spell so that if Gon used it on "N***" instead of "Magnetic Force", he and any friend of his would be sent to Kite.

Intertextuality and References

In Chapter 113 of One-Day Outside Pass Foreman, an in-universe comedy spinoff of mangaka Fukumoto Nobuyuki's Kaiji, main character Ootsuki dreams that his friend Isakawa casts "Accompany" off to 'Magic City', which takes Ootsuki and Isakawa to another of Ootsuki's dreams. The two characters magically fly into the sky immediately after "Accompany" is 'cast'.
